lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY firewall lifecycle (Wave 2, NOT runtime-proven). A firewall is an attribute of a vpc/igw/direct-connect (created implicitly with firewall_enabled:true), NOT a standalone create endpoint — there is no POST /v1/firewalls, only POST /v1/firewalls/rules (needs an existing firewall_id) and PUT /v1/firewalls/{firewall_id}. We have no cheap way to mint a firewall_id in this fragment, so every write is an OPTIONAL probe using a builtin {unique} token in the id path segment: the engine fires the call ({unique} normalizes to catalog '*' and is a builtin so the validator accepts it), records 400/403/404, and counts coverage without a false failure or an undefined-placeholder error. The createfirewallrule body references firewall_id:{unique} (a fake id; expected 4xx). Bodies from data/api_docs.json request_example: createfirewallrule wraps the rule in {firewall_id, firewall_rule:{...}}; setfirewall takes {flavor_name, loggable}; setfirewallrule takes a bare rule body. order_rule_id omitted (an existing rule id we don't have) so order_direction dropped too. createfirewallrule response envelope is $.firewall_rule.id. Promote to a real create->delete chain once a firewall-bearing resource (igw/direct-connect) is wired in as a prerequisite. Covers all 4 firewall WRITE ops: POST /v1/firewalls/rules, PUT /v1/firewalls/rules/{firewall_rule_id}, DELETE /v1/firewalls/rules/{firewall_rule_id}, PUT /v1/firewalls/{firewall_id}.
